sutta: the buddha said monks there are these 5 powers what 5 the power of faith the power of diligence the power of mindfulness or recollection the power of concentration and the power of wisdom these are the 5 powers and where monks is the power of faith to be seen in the 4 factors of stream winning there is the power of faith to be seen and where monks is the power of diligence to be seen in the foreright efforts there is the power of diligence to be seen and where monks is the power of mindfulness or recollection to be seen in the 4 intense states of recollection sati patana there is the power of mindfulness to be seen and where monks is the power of concentration to be seen in the 4 janas there is the power of concentration to be seen and where monks is the power of wisdom to be seen in the 4 aryan truths there the power of wisdom is to be seen these monks are the 5 powers that's the end of the sutan now the first 1 the buddha talks about the faith and sadha or confidence buddha says is this to be seen in the 4 factors of stream winning what are the 4 factors of stream winning the first 1 is the faith in the buddha unshakable faith in the enlightenment of the buddha the second 1 is the unshakable faith or confidence in the dhamma the third is the confidence of faith in the sangha and the fourth is [Music] the factor of virtue silla having pure sila these are the 4 factors of assota panna so then the power of faith is to be seen and whereas monks is about diligence to be seen in the full right efforts what are for right efforts i think if some of you have remember we talked about the full-ride efforts earlier in the chapter of the force where the full-ride efforts refer to the effort to restrain to abandon to develop and to nurture to restrain is to restrain unwholesome thoughts which have not arisen we restrain them from arising the effort to abandon is to abandon those unwholesome states of thoughts which have arisen and then the effort to develop is to develop unarisen wholesome states or wholesome thoughts and the effort to mature is to mature or develop further wholesome states that have already arisen wholesome or skillful states and that is the power of diligence that means we have to always be mindful of the state of our mind to see whether we have wholesome thoughts or some states of mind and where is the power of recollection to be seen in the foreign states of recollection this sati patana sati patana there are different translations for it firstly the word satiri remember the last time we talked on the sutas the last the buddha defines the ability to remember and call to mind what was said and done long ago remember and call to mind what was said and long and done long ago that is the real definition of satina and there's about 8 sutas that repeats this definition and so i you remember i said that if you use the word mindfulness then you have to remember that it is not so much the mindfulness of what is happening day to day what is sense at the sixth sense doors but to remember and to remember what to remember the body and the nature of the body and the mind that it is not i not me not mine and so that is ati and then patana comes from to my interpretation patana comes from 2 words by we look into the pali dictionary means setting forth and also going beyond so going beyond also refers to extreme extreme condition intense [Music] extreme or intense so the word power can be used for extremely intense tana literally means standing still and because of that is also used for a condition a state a situation so the word the pathana can mean extreme condition or extreme state or intense state intense condition that is why my mind tana means an intense state so sati patana means an intense state of recollection or intense state of mindfulness and this sati patana is closely linked to the janas why do we say that sati patana is closely linked to the janas because of 2 sutas in the sanghita nikaya the verbal anuruddha the anuruddha was asked what is the cause of his psychic power and he said that the cause of his psychic power is sati patana normally when we refer to psychic power we associate it with the jhanas intense state of jaanas deep states of concentration i mean and here he says that it is in a way synonymous with the janas and this is again can be seen in another suta where it is stated that the sign of samadhi is sati patana or the sign of jana is sati patana in other words when you have the jhanas or you have perfect concentration then you automatically have sati patana that is why in the suttas in the samyutta nikaya the buddha said cultivate satipatana but after you have attained satipatana the buddha said abandon the practice of sati patana that's something to be found in the sutas so before you attain satikatana you practice to attain it and when you get it you also get the samadhi janas and once you have attained the jhanas you don't have to make an effort to be intensely mindful it becomes automatically a person with the jhanas becomes naturally mindful and then the power of concentration is to be seen in the 4 janus and the last time the the last time we we talked on the sutas we defined the 4 janas and the power of wisdom is to be seen the 4 aryan truths when you have wisdom you will understand the 4 aryan fruits so that's the end of the suture now we come to suta number
